creator: tj+freebsd_bugs@a13.fr
text:
hello,

(In reply to Pedro F. Giffuni from comment #7)
> If it's your first port then it's certainly a brave *good* effort.
> FreeCAD is/was not easy.

  thanks, I did submit another but it's a ruby gem so piece of cake
compared to this :-)

> I just means it builds.. although I am not happy about how it was achieved.
> I am not a CMake expert.

  Sorry for my initial question, I thought it meant something specific
related to ports, now I understand what you meant.

  About CMake, the hack, and hier, I think that maybe in my attempt
for 0.16 I worked in this direction (with patches to CMake files and
some python sources) and I think I complied to what was asked in
porter's guide or other resources I checked at the end. Maybe it's
still applicable to 0.17, I'm building and checking your last attempt
in PR 212251 currently, and will see if I can "improve" this.


> Upstream is careful to note that this is under development and not ready for general use. Working with the upstream version has better chance of working on the long run. I looked at the patches in this port and they, or a variation of them, are already in the upstream version.

  I was not yet ready to make this step (and didn't know which
specific commit to take), but just saw about variations of patches
upstream, this alone remove a lot of work!

  I'll post in 212251 if I find an interesting change to make.
